Hi.
Hey, Aida.
Hi, Aida.
Just to check really quickly, have we showed...
Would you do your scrunch dashboard yet?
I don't think so.
I'm not sure.
Okay, probably not then if you're not, if you can't remember, because I took a look at the previous agendas that we had, and I don't think I shared it with you yet.
So we can do that today.
Thank you.
Okay, looks like we have almost everyone here, I think we're just waiting on Praveen.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Let me ping him very much.
Thank you.
I was just thinking about it.
Thank you.
All right.
Hi
Hi sorry I'm late.
Are we reviewing a doctor?
Hi, no, just waiting for you to join.
Yeah, thank you.
No, just a quick disclaimer, I'm camera off today because I'm dealing with an allergic reaction on my face and I'm just not camera ready right now.
So I'm here, I'm alert, I'm listening, but I'm just, can't be on camera today.
Okay, well, sorry to hear that and hopefully it clears up soon.
Yeah, it's a lot better today.
I'm hoping by the end of the week it clears up.
It's a new sunscreen that I tried.
should not do that.
All right, yeah, so thanks everyone for joining.
So I know that there's some, there are a couple of things that we want to discuss on the PSEO side, but I wanted to just give you a quick content update first.
So the three articles that we delivered last week.
They are ready to publish.
Jose has reviewed them.
He said that they looked good straight out the box.
He just added some more internal links, which is always good.
And he did say that he was going to be staging and publishing them on Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday, which works for us.
I just wanted to note that for each of the ready-to-publish blocks, we have a cover image that you can download from Airtable, so you don't have to generate your own.
So that's available for you to download.
And we also have the meta titles, meta descriptions, and URL slug in these columns at Airtable as well.
We are working on five new blogs this week.
So these are the five that are pretty much done.
I just need to read and edit them.
So, they're going to be ready for you by the end of the week as well, and I'm going to tag Jose once they're ready.
Did you have any other feedback or comments, thoughts on the content so far?
Not me.
Everything on my end as well.
Okay, thanks, Jose.
I did, do you need help staging those articles?
Because we can do that if you want us to.
I'm okay with staging them.
Sometimes, I think, because the schedule is, like, needs to remain decently flexible, as to, like, when we publish which articles, I think I could take it on my end.
But if I do need it, I'll ping you guys.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, that works.
Great.
All right, awesome.
Can I, just one, sorry, one quick question.
So, are there specific keyword searchers that we're trying to move the needle?
So, I'm
On for these articles, and if so, okay, keyword, got it.
Yeah, so when you go to, like, ready for review, if you just go to buy status, you can find the keywords here and the data.
So these are all the ones that have been approved to start.
So the keyword difficulty is here.
Let me expand this.
And the volume is here.
You'll notice that it's a mix of, like, higher volume and lower volume keywords.
The lower volume ones are the ones that are most close to your product.
And the ones that are super low volume, they could be, like, competitor, for example, like, competitor comparisons.
But they are high intent, and it's important to close those content gaps.
And we also, but we also have, like, a fair amount of, like, top of the funnel content with higher volumes.
And we will be adding additional.
Конечно.
And all content to approve to start, but they're all optimized for specific keywords.
Gotcha.
And then for tracking these over time, it like, are these in the Looker dashboard, the Looker report somewhere or?
Yes.
So once we publish the blog posts, we will upload the URLs to the Looker dashboard.
We're actually doing that today.
So they should start showing up in the Looker report pretty soon on the cluster page.
I think that's what it's called.
Cohort.
No, sorry.
It's the cohort page.
So each one of these URLs, you're going to be able to see a breakdown of traffic by cluster.
And then you're also going to be able to see the different URLs.
Awesome.
then last question, remind me how long, we said, like, how long does it take to move the needle?
With that?
Was it like three months that we expect to start seeing some results for these?
It really, let me actually take a look at your, okay, with an authority score of 45, I expect us to start bringing in traffic within four to six weeks.
It usually takes around six weeks to see, like, the full impact of a blog.

So for the first, like, couple of weeks, a blog post might be changing search positions pretty rapidly because Google's algorithm is testing it out for different keywords and seeing the engagement rate and things like that.

And then once it finalizes that analysis, that usually takes around four to six weeks, and that's when you can see, okay, this blog post is, like, going to rank on page two or it's going to rank on page one.
Um, but with a higher authority score, I mean, as soon as we publish a blog post, um, we should, you know, be able to see that it's indexed within about a week.
We should be able to see that it's indexed, that it's getting impressions, and maybe even some, uh, clicks.
So, um, let me actually, um, I'll take a look at your Google Search Console dashboard and just see if the first blog we've published so far, if it's been indexed.
Um, I can do that during this call while you're, um, discussing the, the PSEO project.
Awesome.
Thank you.
All right.
Sorry.

One last one is, um, so will we at some point be, like, covering, kind of, the reporting side in this meeting, or do you guys send us, like, a recap so that we're all kind of on the same page over time with, with how we're trending with, um, SERPs and all that?
Yeah.
Yeah.
So, totally.
No, uh, performance reporting is definitely a part of the weekly meetings.
Um, we just, you know, wait for a couple of weeks to ramp up.
With the publishing to be able to bring you some solid numbers.
I was going to walk you through your scrunch dashboard today for your AI visibility.

But yeah, the performance reporting, is a part of the weekly meetings, especially once you go into phase two and you really start publishing, like, on a very regular basis and you have, like, we have a pretty big batch of content published.
That's when we really cover it every week.
Okay.
Makes sense.
Thanks.
Yeah.
Perfect.
I, I'm not, Erik, do you want to discuss the PSEO project before I get into scrunch?
I'm sure if you have any updates there, we can cover that before I get into the walkthrough.
Yeah.
So we were just chatting in Slack about some of the different kind of proposed content for the page.
One of them being, you know, relevant case studies and given low coverage for different languages.
Yes.
Okay.
Okay.
Thank
It may not make sense to include case studies on the page itself.
We can replace that with, instead of case studies, of use cases.
So kind of proposed ways of using speech-to-text regardless of the language.
So that'll be kind of a little bit of a lower lift as well from not having to have specific data sources or content that we have to pull in.
So that's something that we could explore.
Other, I think, relevant content would just be, like, you know, highlighting any compliance or accuracy or different kind of dedicated support that you guys provide.
Kind of, like, trusted for mission-critical transcription and then having different, you know, Deepgram-specific content there.

But I think it's more of rethinking kind of what we lay out on the content is something I was going to go back to the drawing board and kind of come up with some proposed sections that we could include.
Yeah, you're talking about the language.
Pages, right?
Yeah, the language pages specifically.
So, yeah, we just had a product launch, rolling off a product launch last week, so I've had a chance to take a closer look at that stuff.
So I had started to draft up, basically, a wireframe doc with suggested panels and copy.
I used Eleven Labs speech text pages as inspiration.

A lot of what they do is they take what's on their existing speech to text, like, general page, and then kind of tailor a lot of the content for language-specific stuff on their language-specific pages.
So I was kind of taking the same or similar approach with our suggested wireframes.
I guess my question, then, is, like, if I draft up the wireframes, and I think we should connect with Patricia.
you.
Who handles our web development and stuff on our team.
So if I were to deliver those, you know, a copy doc with suggested, like, wireframes and panels to you, what are the next steps in the workflow?
Yep.
So our team has, like, a basically intake form for PSEO.
We just need to get a better understanding of kind of how the page would be structured from a data perspective.
So, you know, header and then markdown, or is there interactive elements in there?
So I think some of that wireframe will answer those questions.
And then from a publishing perspective for data CMS, we'll have to figure out kind of what the, we'll need an API key to kind of figure out what automated publishing will look like.
And so that's all stuff that I would just need to provide to our team.
And then some of the other questions around code snippets, it sounds like we may want to pull back a little bit on providing code snippets for various languages and just kind of have a...
A link out, similar to Eleven Labs, like link to the developer docs page.
So it's just most of those, like how much interactivity, what's the data structure of the page, and the auto-publishing piece that our team will need to understand.
And then we can kind of get back to you on what we think from an engineering capacity it'll take us to get that stuff live.
Okay.
Yeah.
I think we should connect with Patricia.
I was checking to see if she could join this call.
Just because she's going to also have, she's going to be able to tell you what data CMS components that could be reused and stuff.
So ideally, like if we can, better to get these pictures sooner rather than later.
So if we can streamline and reuse existing components where applicable, there may be one interactive component that needs to be modified.
The rest might just be, like, copy and linking out to help tolerate things.
But, yeah, let me take the first stab in sending you some stuff.
I'll paste it in the Slack.
And then I do think that we need to connect with Pritizia because she knows all the information about RCMS and how your team could, you basically replicate and clone and work that process.
Wonderful.
Yeah, and I'll bring on somebody from our engineering team just to kind of stop playing Metal Man and get the two sources connected with them and Pritizia.
All right.
Sounds good.
Also, Delano had a comment in the chat, by the way.
Oh, yeah.
So, basically, before we get to the, like, end of the meeting, we kind of need to have a chat around how, as we prune AI apps and LearnPages over the next couple of weeks, queincuses...
Yeah.
So, You're Thank

It will definitely affect our organic traffic, and even though we are measuring it as AI apps and non-AI apps right now, for the direct traffic or just organic traffic in general, as the coming weeks kind of go and we start pruning them, that number will start to drop until, of course, you know, four or six weeks down the line, it starts to come back up again.

So, there is a concern that I've been reached out to about how, when the numbers start to drop, although it's required that we prune these pages, we kind of need to make it clear to the business that this makes sense in a way, by showing that, hey, the pages that perform, are performing as well or better, while the prune pages are actually overall supporting the growth of the search engine optimization and all of that.
So, we kind of need to have a little chat about it, so we can probably have it to a day once you guys are done chatting about the rest of the things.
Okay, I think, I mean, relevant, irrelevant traffic, I think all, I think we can say, with the exception of, actually, just let me share my screen really quickly.

Um, so, when we look at, um, the content audit that we did for you, um, if you take a look at all of the pages that we recommend pruning, and then we look at, um, like the MQLs that were influenced and that were rooted, uh, by the, the blog article, um, I think it helps to kind of look at that as, um, an important, measure, so wherever you pulled this data from, I think it's important to just keep tracking, I'm not sure what, um, platform.

You're using to track this data, but I would really look at MQLs overall on your website and on these pages because we want the content that we're publishing to drive more qualified leads to your website.

In terms of the traffic that you're going to lose by removing some of these blog posts that are just about idioms or these AI apps that really aren't relevant, that traffic is more, how do I say this?
Because it looks good as traffic goes, like you get traffic from it, but most of it is not exactly very valuable from a business perspective.
So I think I can prep maybe even like a Notion doc for you if you want, if it's something that you're discussing internally and you would like to have this like as a resource.
to share with people, I think that could maybe be helpful for you to have.
Yeah, that would be helpful.

Also, in general, just to kind of get an idea around this, because the fact that the, I mean, the pages we are pruning, like, although they do have traffic, it's not really influencing the MQLs, as you mentioned, would it mean that when we do eventually prune them, would we see a huge drop in the traffic we see coming into the page?
Or would it be something like a minor change that we'll probably pick up because of the other blog articles we are publishing over the next couple weeks as well?
It depends on how many we prune.
So all of the pruned pages, they will get a redirect.
So the traffic is going to be redirected, at least direct one is going to be redirected to other parts of your website.
So overall, it really depends on how many we want to prune.
I think if we prune, like, 20 to 30.
Blogs, I don't think the impact will be as significant than, you know, just removing like a whole section of your website.
Okay, so that means over the next couple of weeks we won't be pruning the entire bunch of it, it'll be in sets, maybe 20 at a time or something like that?
Yeah, yeah, exactly.

I think especially with regards to the blog, we want to, you know, we want to take a look at all of the ones that we have recommended pruning, and then we want to decide which pages we're going to be sending that traffic to.
So that's going to be also a part of the process.
And the list, the spreadsheet of the pruning, the audit that you guys, that's in our, in the Notion, shared Notion, access?
Okay, I'll take a closer look at it.
And then Delano, I believe we, they haven't done any audit on any, on AI Apps or AI Glossary yet, so that's not covered under this.
I believe, so that's something we'll look at for later.
Another thing in general that just came up was figuring out like what our quality sessions are.
Like, of course, the ones that influence the high MQAs are more high in quality, but there are certain pages in the learn and general product pages that brings in a ton of traffic to us.
So, is there any recommended or suggested way that we can directly track the performance for those pages as a group?
For example, right now we are doing it as in AI apps versus non-AI apps.
So, the learn pages come down to non-AI apps.
But as we kind of start pooling and get rid of the stuff over AI apps over the coming weeks, at some point at least, that means we wouldn't need that segmentation anymore.
And we're looking at the entire website as one set of like organic.

So, is there a way we can, like, group the specific content or pages that we can, like, let's say, call quality sessions because they have a higher influence, and just track those pages as to how they perform over the next couple of weeks as we make these changes, just to make sure that, you know, although we are pruning the pages and stuff, the performing ones, the key performing pages are performing as equally or better, just to make that separation.

So, in case someone comes and asks us, you guys are deleting random stuff on the page, we can just directly point it out and be like, hey, no, like, we are removing it, but also the pages that were doing well are doing better because we are removing this, because the traffic is now being directed to somewhere that actually makes more sense.
Then an idiom page.
How would you recommend going about showcasing that in terms of like explaining the data for that, I guess?
If this might be a bit, you know, a lengthy conversation, I'm totally happy for you all to go back, have a chat and give us an idea.
It's just that it's something that some stakeholders brought up and I want to kind of clear out before the actual question starts rising up.
Yeah, no, no, I totally get what you mean.
And it is important to track this.
I think the best idea would be for me to go back and talk to one of our directors to see what would be the best way to measure this, because there are a couple of options.
And there are things that we can measure even, you know, in the Looker report.
But I do want to go back and see kind of what the best practice is.
I mean, there are things like measuring, you know, I mean, setting up exploration reports in GA4 so you can see where the traffic.

Because going, for example, from your blog, but yeah, I think we want to see overall, like the one thing that we do want to see is after pruning, if you're planning on pruning like the AI apps and like doing more heavier content removal on the site, we do want to see an overall lift to the organic traffic in the period of time.
So to the overall website.
And I think we could, yeah, I think that's, this is something I can discuss with, with Jacob, who's a managing director here.
And then, then I can get back to you, because there are a couple of options right now.
But, you know, I don't want to suggest anything without chatting to him first.
Yeah, makes sense to me.
Feel free to reach out if any questions or, yeah, open to ideas.
Of course.
Yeah, and I actually just wanted to show you really quickly.
Um, in Looker, in your Looker, uh, performance report.
Um, so we have updated it with just the one blog post that we've published so far.
Um, and it's gotten, um, it has, uh, 37 sessions so far.
Um, and the engagement rate is about 48%, which is pretty good.
Um, it means around, like, 50% of the people are, are engaging with, with the blog and staying on the page for more than 10 seconds, um, or scrolling.
Um, which is good, um, as we publish this week, we're going to update this, so we're going to start seeing, like, additional pillars here.
This is, um, the content cluster that the voice activity detection blog is under.
Um, and obviously, the performance changes here, mean, the non-growthx URLs, they are very, very dominant.
So, because we have just started publishing, but once we ramp up, we are going to be able to start seeing, um, our URLs here as well.
Uh, is there anything we need for, oh, I'll ask you to start publishing these.
I'll
As we start publishing these, should we just be promoting these through our channels, through our usual methods, or is there anything special we should be doing for promoting these content?

I think, yeah, I think if you could add, like, whenever you're publishing new content, or if you're refreshing content, I think you can just, like, add internal links, that would be helpful, just for, like, an SEO boost and from an indexing perspective.
Okay, so that's within the articles that you guys are publishing?
Yeah, just, yeah, within our articles, but if you're also publishing something that was written in-house, you can also add these links.
Okay, I see, so linking out to these articles.
Yeah, yeah, that would be probably most helpful from an SEO perspective.
Got it, makes sense.
Yeah, all right, so we have a minute left, so I...

I won't be able to do your scrunch walkthrough today, but I will send you a PDF of the report on Slack, and then I can just show you, like, give you, like, a quick breakdown, and then I can give you, like, an actual walkthrough next week, so you can kind of see what are the prompts that we're tracking, and you can also suggest prompts of your own.
Okay, excellent.
Cool.
All right, well, thank you so much for your time.
I'll follow up in Slack with some additional notes, and, Delano, I will get back to you regarding the performance tracking before our next call.
Thank you.
All right.
Thank you.
See next week.
See you week.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Deepgram and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, SEO performance, and product-specific SEO (PSEO) initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content production is on track with 3 articles ready to publish and 5 more in progress](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
  - [PSEO strategy for language pages is being refined, focusing on use cases rather than case studies](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=765.0)
  - [Content pruning strategy needs careful consideration to balance traffic loss with improved quality](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)
  - [New performance tracking methods are needed to demonstrate the value of content strategy changes](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1386.0)

## Topics

### Content Production Update

  - [3 articles ready for publishing (Wed-Fri)](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
  - [Cover images, meta titles, descriptions, and URL slugs available in Airtable](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=379.0)
  - [5 new blogs in progress, to be ready by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=406.0)
  - [Jose to handle staging, maintaining flexible publishing schedule](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=443.0)
  - [Keyword targeting mix: high-volume (top of funnel) and low-volume (product-specific, high intent)](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)

### SEO Performance Tracking

  - [Looker dashboard to show URL-specific performance within 4-6 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)
  - [First published blog showing 37 sessions, 48% engagement rate](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1616.0)
  - [Weekly performance reporting to ramp up as more content is published](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=696.0)
  - [Scrunch dashboard walkthrough postponed, PDF report to be shared via Slack](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1740.0)

### PSEO Strategy for Language Pages

  - [Shift from case studies to use cases due to low language coverage](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=765.0)
  - [Hasan drafting wireframes inspired by Eleven Labs' approach](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=863.0)
  - [Need to connect with Patricia for web development insights](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0)
  - [Focus on reusing existing CMS components where possible](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1008.0)
  - [Potential for one interactive component, rest mainly copy and external links](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1008.0)

### Content Pruning Strategy

  - [Concern about organic traffic drop as AI apps and LearnPages are pruned](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)
  - [Need to demonstrate value of pruning to stakeholders](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1066.0)
  - [Suggestion to prune in sets (e.g., 20-30 blogs at a time) to minimize impact](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1268.0)
  - [Redirects to be implemented for pruned pages](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1299.0)
  - [Importance of tracking MQLs and quality sessions post-pruning](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1386.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Hasan to share PSEO wireframes and copy doc in Slack](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1026.0)
  - [Erik to provide PSEO intake form details to team](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=905.0)
  - [Schedule meeting with Patricia to discuss CMS components and publishing process](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=986.0)
  - [Aida to prepare Notion doc explaining pruning strategy and its business impact](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1249.0)
  - [Aida to consult with Jacob on best practices for measuring quality sessions post-pruning](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1386.0)
  - [Team to implement internal linking strategy for new and existing content](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1680.0)
  - [Aida to send PDF of Scrunch report via Slack and provide full walkthrough next week](https://fathom.video/share/mbH6h8mrNbzrtbuLYTgPgkXxsjs1oBPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1740.0)

action_items.description: Stage + publish 3 new articles Wed/Thu/Fri; use provided cover images, meta titles/descriptions, URL slugs
Update Looker dashboard w/ new blog URLs for performance tracking
Draft wireframes for language-specific pages, inspired by Eleven Labs; include suggested panels and copy
Connect w/ Patricia re web dev components for language pages; discuss dataCMS reusability
Provide API key + publishing details to team for automated PSEO publishing
Send wireframe drafts to Erik via Slack for PSEO intake
Prep Notion doc explaining traffic impact of pruning pages, focus on MQLs vs raw traffic
Discuss w/ Jacob best practices for measuring quality sessions post-pruning
